articles.title: SEC ALLOWS BROKER-DEALERS TO COUNT STABLECOINS AS REGULATORY CAPITAL
articles.summary: The SEC issued new guidance allowing US broker-dealers to count stablecoin holdings as regulatory capital with just a 2% haircut, effectively treating 98% of these digital dollar assets as cash equivalents on balance sheets. This marks a significant departure from previous treatment of crypto assets, which typically faced much higher capital charges or were excluded entirely from regulatory capital calculations. The policy shift could accelerate adoption of tokenized securities and on-chain settlement infrastructure among traditional financial institutions by removing a major capital efficiency barrier for firms holding stablecoins to facilitate blockchain-based transactions.

articles.title: WHITE HOUSE SETS MARCH 1 DEADLINE ON STABLECOIN REWARDS DISPUTE
articles.summary: The White House set a March 1 deadline to resolve the stablecoin rewards dispute, currently the primary obstacle blocking passage of the Clarity Act. The administration held its third closed-door crypto meeting with industry stakeholders, including Ripple, the Blockchain Association, the Crypto Council for Innovation, and major banking trade associations. Lawmakers remain split on whether stablecoins should be permitted to offer yield to holders, with the issue now representing one of the last major sticking points in comprehensive crypto legislation.

articles.title: DFLOW MCP: INSTANT TRADING ACCESS FOR AGENTS
articles.summary: DFlow launched an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that gives AI agents structured access to its complete Solana trading API documentation, replacing search-based integration with direct canonical queries across Trading, Metadata, and Proof APIs. Agents can now programmatically detect prediction market arbitrage opportunities (such as YES + NO asks totaling less than $1), stream websocket data for automated execution, and debug trade failures by inspecting live API specs. The server integrates with OpenClaw, Claude, Cursor, and other AI development environments, enabling agents to route orders through specific DEXes, track outcome token balances, and build automated trading strategies without manual API documentation parsing.

articles.title: A NEW, UNIFIED STACK FOR BASE CHAIN
articles.summary: Base is evolving from the OP Stack to a unified, Base-operated tech stack, with node operators needing to migrate to Base's client releases instead of following Optimism's releases in the coming months as part of the transition. The move aims to achieve three objectives: doubling the upgrade cadence from three to six hard forks annually for faster shipping, reducing cognitive overhead by simplifying the codebase to be understandable by a single developer rather than maintaining complex external dependencies across multiple teams and repositories, and accelerating deployment of high-impact changes ahead of Ethereum's L1 to inform the mainnet roadmap. The upcoming Base V1 hard fork will implement client consolidation requiring node operator migration, transition from Optimistic proofs to TEE/ZK proofs for faster finality, and support Ethereum's Fusaka upgrade, with future releases including Block Access Lists, new transaction types, op-code repricing, and economic changes aligned with Ethereum's roadmap.

articles.title: HOW TO FIX TOKENS: PROFIT FROM HOLDING, NOT SELLING
articles.summary: Crypto tokens are structurally misaligned because holders profit by selling into others rather than by holding, creating an exit game of insider unlocks and retail dilution. There should be a model where 100% of protocol revenue is governed by tokenholders and distributed directly, shifting incentives toward long-term ownership and revenue growth. With improving infrastructure and a rare regulatory window in 2025, explicit revenue-sharing tokens (rather than buybacks, burns, or inflationary staking) should define the next era of crypto design before policymakers lock in rules around the current extractive model.

articles.title: IS YOUR PAYMENT INFRASTRUCTURE READY FOR AGENTIC PAYMENTS?
articles.summary: AI agents could drive $500 billion in US commerce by 2030, requiring payment systems to evolve from manual clicks to autonomous delegation. Success depends on ledger-grade guarantees to prevent double spending and ensure auditability during high-concurrency automated retries.

articles.title: OPTIMISM'S UGLY YEAR
articles.summary: Coinbase is moving Base from the OP Stack to an independent unified stack, jeopardizing the Optimism Superchain. Base generates 97% of Superchain revenue, leaving the ecosystem financially vulnerable. The OP token is down 97% from its peak, and ether.fi recently committed to migrating 300,000 accounts.

articles.title: ‘THIS IS WRONG,' VITALIK BUTERIN SLAMS WEB4 VISION OF SUPERINTELLIGENT AI
articles.summary: Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin rejected Sigil Wen's Web4 vision, citing its reliance on centralized providers like OpenAI and Anthropic. Buterin argues that removing humans from AI feedback loops risks irreversible anti-human outcomes. He emphasizes that technology should prioritize human agency over autonomous, self-replicating systems.

articles.title: AGENTIC COMMERCE LANDSCAPE
articles.summary: Artemis maps the current agentic commerce landscape with an infographic covering the major protocols and infrastructure layers enabling AI agent-to-agent transactions.

articles.title: OPENAI INTRODUCES EVMBENCH
articles.summary: OpenAI's EVMbench is a new benchmark that measures how well AI agents can detect, exploit, and patch high-severity smart contract vulnerabilities.

articles.title: BITDEER SELLS ITS LAST BTC, TREASURY DROPS TO ZERO
articles.summary: Bitdeer sold its remaining 943.1 BTC in one week (while mining 189.8 BTC and selling that too), completing an eight-week drawdown from roughly 2,000 BTC at year-end.

articles.title: SPECIALIZED AI DETECTS 92% OF REAL-WORLD DEFI EXPLOITS
articles.summary: Specialized AI agents detected 92% of vulnerabilities in 90 exploited DeFi contracts, totaling $96.8 million.
